title: Logos: Build the Parallel
description: Private-by default technology to reclaim civil society. Logos is the decentralised infrastructure for building parallel systems.
heading: Build the Parallel

sections.headline: Build the Parallel
sections.body: Logos is the private-by-default technology stack for parallel societies. Built for a world where existing systems have stopped working. Built for a world where we are in control.
sections.bodySecondary: Basecamp is the tool for living outside existing systems.

cards.title: Build
cards.description: Explore programmes and resources to help you build your cyberstate.

cards.title: Node Programme
cards.description: The Node Programme is for anyone who wants to join our movement to revitalise civil society using decentralised technologies.

cards.title: Circles
cards.description: Local chapters are at the heart of our movement. Learn civil organising, share skills, and forge new connections.

sections.headline2: Logos is for people who are done waiting for permission.

contributions.label: Contributors
contributions.body: An open source community of builders, researchers, and technologists.

nodeOperators.label: Node Operators
nodeOperators.body: A decentralised network of independent Node operators ensuring Logos is secure.

winnableIssues.label: Winnable Issues
winnableIssues.body: Local issues that Circles identify and solve – from privacy tech to community funding.

circles.label: Circles
circles.body: Local chapters of activists and change seekers solving real world issues.

sections.title: Choose your path
sections.kicker: The right response to broken systems is not to reform them but to build alongside them, and eventually, beyond them.
sections.body: Take action to reclaim agency and find your purpose.

build.title: Build
build.body: Build privacy-first, decentralised applications. Read the docs, learn the stack, write code.

operate.title: Operate
operate.body: Run a node. Decentralise the network. Secure Logos. Setup takes minutes.

activism.title: Organise
activism.body: Humanity evolved to organise. Some of our biggest problems lie in the breakdown of social trust, culture, and meaning. Join the Logos Movement to rebuild civil society together.

sections.headline: We get to decide what comes next.
sections.headline3: Or exit and build the alternative.
bodyParts: Logos is the private-by-default technology stack

sections.heading:
Civil society is in decline. Our institutions are failing.
Reform from within cannot reverse this.
sections.headingMobile:
Civil society is in decline.
Our institutions are failing.
Reform from within
cannot reverse this.

debt.title: Debt
debt.subtitle: The bill lands on us
debt.body: We were born into a debt we never authorised and have inherited consequences of decisions we did not make. As interest compounds, the total grows. The bill lands on us.
facts: Global government debt has climbed to a record $111 trillion in 2025, rising by $8.3 trillion in a single year.
The average millennial in the US has 30% less wealth than the average Boomer by age 35.
3.4 billion people now live in countries that spend more on interest payments than on either health or education.
Just 27% of Gen Z and 55% of millennials own a home, compared to 80% of Baby Boomers.

surveillance.title: Surveillance
surveillance.subtitle: Privacy is no longer the default
surveillance.body: We are the most watched generation in human history. Online activity, location, and communications are routinely collected, stored, and sold, often without meaningful consent. Privacy is no longer a default condition.
facts: There are now ~1 billion surveillance cameras installed worldwide. More than 1 for every 8 people on Earth.
75% of police forces globally now have access to facial recognition technology.
Governments now legally purchase massive quantities of citizens' personal data from commercial brokers.

corruption.title: Corruption
corruption.subtitle: The rules are for sale
corruption.body: Policy is increasingly shaped by those who can pay to influence it. Lobbying, campaign donations, and direct access give organised money a structural advantage over the public interest.
facts: Billionaires are now estimated to be 4,000 times more likely to hold political office than ordinary citizens.
In the US alone, federal lobbying smashed records in 2025, with corporations and interest groups spending $5.08 billion in a single year to influence Washington.
Two-thirds of Americans (67%) now believe their federal government is "corrupt", and trust in government has crashed to just 17%.

stagnation.title: Stagnation
stagnation.subtitle: The system protects itself
stagnation.body: Since 1979, productivity has grown while wages stay flat. The output is real, but the gains are captured upstream by top earners and capital owners, not wages. This has been done systematically over decades.
facts: The 12 richest billionaires now own more wealth than the poorest half of humanity — 4 billion people.
Millennials work in an economy 70% more productive than when boomers were the same age — yet their median compensation has barely moved.

sections.eyebrow: A unified ecosystem. Private-by-default. Built for real life.
sections.title: The Logos Technology Stack

basecamp.title: Basecamp
basecamp.body: The fully local launcher for the Logos stack, running all modules on your device from a unified interface.

pillars.title: Storage
pillars.body: Privacy-preserving file sharing and retrieval using content-addressed (CID-based) data. In future, this module will support decentralised file storage.

details.title: Logos Execution Zone (LEZ)
details.body: Developers can deploy programs, run AMMs, transfer tokens, and build financial primitives with built-in privacy.

details.title: Data Availability and Consensus: Cryptarchia
details.body: A private proof-of-stake consensus mechanism where validator identities and stake amounts remain hidden.

pillars.title: User Modules
pillars.body: Anyone can build modules that plug into the same IPC infrastructure.

sections.networkingTitle: The Networking Stack: Discovery, Peering, and Mixnet
sections.networkingDescription: This layer handles how Logos nodes find each other, establish connections, and communicate.
sections.foundationDescription: A runtime that handles the essential primitives every decentralised application needs.

sections.title: Start building.
sections.body: Everything you need to start building privacy-first, decentralised applications. Read the docs, study the stack, write code, explore builder programmes.
sections.ideas: Starter Issues and Community Ideas Repo

Logos is the technology stack that makes it the default.

secure.title: Secure, private communications.
secure.body: Resilient, private messaging for coordination in hostile environments.

money.title: Private, censorship-resistant money.
money.body: Private financial networks for storing and moving value without surveillance.

archives.title: Permanent, decentralised archives.
archives.body: Decentralised network for storing knowledge, culture, and history beyond the reach of any single centralised actor.

donations.title: Anonymous donations and mutual aid.
donations.body: Private payment network for funding people and causes without putting parties at risk.

title.rest: was a two-day event in Lisbon where music, art, and technology converge.

sections.title:
Basecamp.
The local launcher
for the Logos stack.
sections.description:
Basecamp is a ready-to-run Logos distribution that you deploy on your own hardware, under your control. From the moment it’s installed, you’re standing on your own ground, not connecting to someone else’s infrastructure.

The Logos runtime runs locally, core modules are loaded, and essential applications — wallet, messenger, file sharing, blockchain explorer — are ready to use from day one.

title.rest: was a two-day event in Lisbon where music, art, and technology converged.

body: Logos is built for those who believe the window for reform has closed. Circles are essential for anyone taking actions with us. If there isn’t a Logos Circle listed in your area, please get in touch with the team.
